title: Inbound Follow: auto-Accept, locked accounts queue to follow_requests
description:
Handle inbound Follow{object: local_actor_uri} from verified remote actor. For unlocked accounts (is_locked=false): create followers row immediately, enqueue Accept{Follow} activity to delivery_queue targeting the follower's inbox. For locked accounts: create follow_requests row instead.

CRITICAL: Mastodon sends Follow where the object field is SOMETIMES a full actor object and SOMETIMES just a URI string. This is the most common bug in AP implementations. Handle both shapes.

**Agent workflow**: Spawn a research subagent to collect Follow activity fixtures from mastodon.social (object as URI) and other servers (object as full object) and verify both shapes. Spawn a parallel subagent to write the Accept activity generation code.

**Test discipline**:
- Test fixture where object is URI string: handled correctly
- Test fixture where object is full object: handled correctly
- Test locked account: follow_requests row created, NO Accept sent
- Test duplicate Follow (same follower, same target): idempotent — don't create duplicate followers row, re-send Accept
- Test Follow from actor not yet in remote_accounts: triggers actor resolution, then proceeds
All fixtures captured from real servers. Never use implementation as its own oracle. Real in-memory SQLite for DB tests.

**Defensive programming**:
- Verify the Follow activity is signed by the same actor in the 'actor' field
- Validate the 'object' value (whether URI or object) refers to a local actor
- Accept activity must be enqueued before returning 202 — don't return success if we can't accept
- No .unwrap() outside test code; all errors are Result<T, AppError>
acceptance_criteria: Follow with object-as-URI handled; Follow with object-as-object handled; locked account goes to follow_requests with no Accept sent; duplicate Follow is idempotent; Accept enqueued to delivery_queue
